summary: Tested Smart Match and Rarity Badge features. All 8 backend API tests passed. Frontend tests passed - 'Rarest First' and 'Most Common' sort options work correctly, RarityBadge displays appropriate badges. Fixed a minor bug in RarityBadge.js where data-testid could fail if label was undefined.

backend_api_tests.test_smart_match_real_artist_title_auto_: PASS - POST /api/records with 'Thriller' by 'Michael Jackson' auto-populated discogs_id, cover_url, and rarity_label
backend_api_tests.test_smart_match_fake_artist_title_no_ma: PASS - POST /api/records with fake artist/title returns discogs_id=None and rarity_label='Unknown'
backend_api_tests.test_smart_match_confidence_check_artist: PASS - High-confidence matching requires both artist AND title to fuzzy-match
backend_api_tests.test_record_with_discogs_id_gets_proper_: PASS - Records with discogs_id get rarity from Discogs community data (not 'Unknown')
backend_api_tests.test_record_without_discogs_id_gets_unkn: PASS - Records without discogs_id correctly get 'Unknown' rarity
backend_api_tests.test_enrich_rarity_sets_unknown_for_no_d: PASS - POST /api/records/enrich-rarity sets 'Unknown' for records without discogs_id
backend_api_tests.test_rarity_thresholds: PASS - calculate_rarity() correctly computes Common/Uncommon/Rare/Very Rare/Ultra Rare/Grail/Obscure based on have/want ratios
backend_api_tests.test_get_records_returns_rarity_label: PASS - GET /api/records includes rarity_label field for client-side sorting

frontend_tests.rarest_first_option: PASS - 'Rarest First' sort option present in dropdown
frontend_tests.most_common_option: PASS - 'Most Common' sort option present in dropdown
frontend_tests.rarity_badges_rendered: PASS - Found 149 rarity badges with data-testid
frontend_tests.sorting_rarest_first: PASS - Shows Uncommon records first (rarest in collection)

critical_code_review_comments: collection.py line 56-79: Smart Match correctly searches Discogs when no discogs_id provided, requires both artist AND title fuzzy-match for confidence
collection.py line 92-104: Rarity calculation correctly sets 'Unknown' for records without discogs_id

rarity.py: calculate_rarity() implements correct thresholds - Grail (ratio>20, have<500), Ultra Rare (ratio>=8 or >20), Very Rare (>=5), Rare (>=3), Uncommon (>=1), Common (<1), Obscure (have<25, want<10)
RarityBadge.js: TIER_CONFIG includes all tiers including 'Unknown' with gray/muted style
CollectionPage.js: SORT_OPTIONS includes 'rarest_first' and 'most_common', sort logic correctly orders by rarity
seed_data_creation: Created test records with 'Thriller' by 'Michael Jackson' for Smart Match testing (cleaned up after tests)
context_for_next_testing_agent: Smart Match and Rarity features fully working. Smart Match requires both artist AND title to fuzzy-match for confidence. Records without discogs_id get 'Unknown' rarity. Collection sorting includes 'Rarest First' (Ultra Rare > Very Rare > Rare > Uncommon > Common > Unknown) and 'Most Common' (reverse). RarityBadge component displays correct tier colors. Fixed minor bug in RarityBadge.js where data-testid used 'label' instead of 'displayLabel'.

smart_match.behavior: When POST /api/records has artist+title but no discogs_id, backend searches Discogs and auto-links if high-confidence match (both artist AND title fuzzy-match)

rarity_badge_fix.behavior: Records without discogs_id get 'Unknown' rarity instead of 'Ultra Rare'

collection_sorting.sort_order_rarest: Grail > Ultra Rare > Very Rare > Rare > Uncommon > Common > Unknown
collection_sorting.sort_order_common: Common > Uncommon > Rare > Very Rare > Ultra Rare > Unknown
